meta.author: ARCHITECT — BMAD A2A
references: archonx/visualization/agent_theater.py (Python event bus — source of WS events)

overview.name: Chess Theater — 3D Agent Orchestration Visualization
overview.description: A Next.js 15 app with Three.js r128 rendering 32 3D chess pieces representing the agent roster. Real-time board state from chess-server WebSocket (port 3300). Agent actions in kernel map to chess piece movements.
overview.location: chess-theater/ (new Next.js app at repo root)
overview.quality_gate: UDEC 8.5/10 minimum — PS5-grade graphics, not pixel trash

technology_stack.shaders: GLSL (custom vertex + fragment shaders for pieces)
technology_stack.styling: Tailwind CSS 3 (minimal — most UI is Three.js canvas)

chess-theater/.next.config.ts: static export for Vercel, WASM support

app/.layout.tsx: Root layout — dark background, no navbar

components/.ChessTheater.tsx: Main Three.js canvas component (useEffect mounts, useRef canvas)
components/.AgentHUD.tsx: Overlay showing active agent name, action, status (HTML over canvas)

scene/.SceneManager.ts: Three.js scene, camera, renderer, lights setup
scene/.PieceFactory.ts: Creates 32 piece meshes from AGENT_ROSTER data
scene/.PieceMaterial.ts: White crew: metallic silver, Black crew: matte obsidian (GLSL)

ws/.EventParser.ts: Parse chess-server WS messages → BoardStateUpdate type

animation/.PieceAnimator.ts: GSAP-style lerp for piece movement (arc trajectory, not linear)
animation/.PieceStates.ts: Idle (breathe pulse), Thinking (glow pulse), Moving (arc fly), Active (bright aura), Done (settle)

types/.agents.ts: AgentPiece, BoardSquare, BoardState, WsMessage, PieceAnimState types

shaders/.piece.vert: Vertex shader — subtle breathing animation via sin(time)
shaders/.piece.frag: Fragment shader — metallic BRDF for white pieces, obsidian for black
shaders/.board.frag: Board square shader — emissive pulse when piece is active
shaders/.aura.frag: Agent aura/glow effect when actively processing

payload.action_type: TASK_START | TASK_COMPLETE | COUNCIL_ENTER | COUNCIL_EXIT | COMMS_SEND | ERROR

MOVING.description: Agent executing action — arc trajectory to target square
MOVING.shader_effect: motion trail via vertex shader (stretch along velocity vector)

ACTIVE.description: Agent is currently executing a task on its square
ACTIVE.shader_effect: radial aura pulse around piece base, cyan (#00BFFF) for white crew, magenta (#FF00FF) for black crew
ACTIVE.board_effect: current square emissive pulse synchronized with piece aura

DONE.description: Agent completed action — brief flash then settle
DONE.shader_effect: quick white flash (0.3s) then return to idle

ERROR.shader_effect: red pulse (#FF0000) 3x rapid flash, then dim to 50% brightness until resolved

camera.controls: OrbitControls — allow rotation and zoom, clamp polar angle 15-75deg
camera.cinematic_mode: Slow auto-rotation at 0.001 rad/frame when idle

board.light_squares: #16213e (slightly lighter navy) — NOT white/black chess (it's the AGENT board)
board.coordinates: a-h, 1-8 inscribed in gold on board edge (small, tasteful)

piece_geometry.king: custom GLTF or lathe geometry — crown silhouette, height 1.2
piece_geometry.queen: lathe geometry — elegant curved form, height 1.1
piece_geometry.rook: BoxGeometry with chamfered top — solid, strong
piece_geometry.knight: SphereGeometry with offset — abstracted horse head silhouette
piece_geometry.fallback: If GLTF loading fails, use geometric primitives (never null/invisible)

hud_overlay.technology: React HTML overlay (position: absolute, pointer-events: none)

active_agent_card.content: Agent name, role, current action, elapsed time
active_agent_card.style: glass card, backdrop-blur, agent crew color border

council_view.content: When council active: White vs Black crew icons + debate round indicator

system_metrics.content: Active agents count, tasks/hr, cost today

ws_status.content: Connection status dot (green/yellow/red) + last event timestamp

performance_requirements.lod: Implement 2 LOD levels per piece: full detail < 10 units, simplified > 10 units from camera
performance_requirements.shadow_maps: Enabled for key light only — shadow map 1024x1024

kernel_bridge.description: How kernel agent actions flow to the Chess Theater frontend
flow: 4. NEW: agent_theater.py forwards event to chess-server via HTTP POST /api/events
6. chess-server broadcasts BoardStateUpdate over WebSocket to all connected Three.js clients
7. Three.js client receives WS message → triggers piece animation

agent_theater_change.change: Add chess_server_url config + forward_to_chess_server() method that POSTs events to chess-server

non_negotiables: All 32 pieces ALWAYS visible (never hidden or errored out)
Active agent immediately identifiable (< 1s visual latency from WS event)
Color palette: dark navy + gold + crew colors — NO default Three.js colors
Typography: Space Grotesk for HUD (Google Fonts, load in Next.js)
Piece materials: metallic shader for white crew, matte obsidian for black crew — NO BasicMaterial
Error states visible (piece dims/flashes red) — never silent failure

mapping.event_type == 'task_start': → chess action TASK_START, animate piece to active
mapping.event_type == 'task_complete': → chess action TASK_COMPLETE, animate piece to done
mapping.event_type == 'council_enter': → chess action COUNCIL_ENTER, show council HUD

builder_notes: Context7 REQUIRED before writing Three.js code: resolve three.js library ID, fetch r128 API docs
Use Three.js ShaderMaterial for piece rendering — NOT MeshStandardMaterial (too generic)
GLTF piece models: generate simple lathe geometries in code for MVP (no external assets required)
WebSocket reconnect MUST be implemented — chess-server restart shouldn't break frontend
Three.js canvas MUST be responsive (ResizeObserver on container, update camera aspect ratio)
DO NOT use Three.js examples/jsm OrbitControls import path — use three/examples/jsm/controls/OrbitControls.js
